content: The School of Computer Science & Engineering at Presidency University offers cutting-edge programmes in computing, AI, data science, and emerging technologies.
Programmes are approved and affiliated with leading professional bodies, providing global academic recognition.

programs.name: B.Tech Computer Science & Engineering

hero.description: Build a strong foundation in computing, AI, and modern software technologies.

overview: The B.Tech in Electronics & Communication Engineering explores the design, development, and deployment of electronic devices, circuits, and communication systems that underpin modern life. From mobile networks and satellite communications to embedded controllers and IoT devices, the programme builds expertise across analogue and digital electronics, signal processing, wireless systems, VLSI technology, and microcontroller-based design. Students gain fluency in both hardware and software aspects of electronic engineering through an integrated curriculum of theory, laboratory practice, and project work.

Bengaluru’s position as a centre for semiconductor manufacturing, telecom R&D, and electronics innovation gives students proximity to major employers and research organisations. The programme incorporates industry-oriented certifications, collaborative projects, and internship placements that connect classroom learning to professional practice. Graduates emerge prepared for roles in telecommunications, semiconductor design, consumer electronics, aerospace, defence, and automation industries worldwide.

faq.q: What is eligibility?
faq.a: 10+2 with PCM.

faq.q: How do international students apply for the B.Tech CSE programme?
faq.a: International applicants should visit the Presidency University International admissions portal or contact the Office of International Affairs directly. The process typically involves submitting academic transcripts, proof of English language proficiency, and relevant entrance examination scores. Admission considers JEE Main, KCET, COMEDK, or the university’s own evaluation test.

faq.q: What entrance examinations are accepted?
faq.a: The university accepts JEE Main, JEE Advanced, Karnataka CET (KCET), COMEDK UGET, and the Presidency University Entrance Examination Test (PUEET). International applicants should check with the admissions team whether alternative qualifications are accepted.

faq.q: What career prospects are available after graduation?
faq.a: Graduates find roles as software developers, data scientists, AI engineers, cybersecurity specialists, cloud architects, and more across multinational IT companies, product firms, start-ups, government research bodies, and consulting organisations. The university’s placement cell maintains strong industry connections with regular on-campus recruitment drives.

faq.q: Is the B.Tech CSE programme in demand globally?
faq.a: Yes, B.Tech in Computer Science & Engineering remains one of the most in-demand degrees worldwide due to the growing need for tech professionals across every industry. Graduates are hired for roles spanning software development, data science, AI, and cybersecurity, with strong salary prospects and global career mobility.

faq.q: Is on-campus accommodation available for international students?
faq.a: Presidency University offers dedicated student housing on its Bengaluru campus. International students are encouraged to contact the Office of International Affairs for information on hostel availability, room types, and the booking process.

programs.name: B.Tech Computer Science & Technology (Big Data)

hero.description: Harness the power of large-scale data to drive decisions that shape industries. This four-year specialisation equips you with the tools and techniques to collect, process, analyse, and visualise complex datasets — preparing you for one of the fastest-growing career fields in global technology.

overview: Data is expanding at an unprecedented pace, and the ability to extract actionable insights from complex datasets is now a core requirement across virtually every industry. The B.Tech in Computer Science & Technology with a specialisation in Big Data is an interdisciplinary programme that fuses principles from statistics, computer science, and domain-specific applications to prepare graduates for roles as qualified data engineers and analysts.

The programme combines foundational computer science with deep expertise in mathematics, statistical analysis, computer programming, and software design — all oriented towards the effective management and interpretation of digital data at scale. Students work with industry-standard tools and platforms including Python, R, Hadoop, Apache Spark, TensorFlow, and advanced analytics frameworks, building competencies that align directly with global employer demand.

faq.q: How does the Big Data specialisation differ from the core CSE programme?
faq.a: While the core CSE programme offers broad coverage across all computing disciplines, the Big Data specialisation provides deep expertise in large-scale data processing, analytics, machine learning, and data visualisation. Students gain hands-on experience with platforms like Hadoop, Spark, and TensorFlow alongside core computing fundamentals.

faq.q: What tools and technologies will I learn?
faq.a: : The programme covers Python, R, SQL, Hadoop, Apache Spark, TensorFlow, cloud analytics platforms (AWS, Google Cloud, Azure), Power BI, Tableau, and various machine learning and deep learning frameworks.

faq.q: What are the eligibility requirements for international applicants?
faq.a: Applicants must have completed 10+2 or equivalent with Physics and Mathematics as compulsory subjects, along with Chemistry or Computer Science or related subjects, with at least 45% marks combined. Entrance through JEE, KCET, COMEDK, or PUEET.

faq.q: Are there industry certification opportunities during the programme?
faq.a: Yes. Students can earn globally recognised certifications including AWS Data Analytics, Google Data Engineer, and TensorFlow Developer credentials alongside their degree.

faq.q: What is the typical career trajectory for graduates?
faq.a: Graduates typically begin as data engineers or analysts, progressing to senior data scientist, lead data architect, or analytics manager roles. The specialisation also provides a strong foundation for postgraduate research in data science and AI.

faq.q: Is on-campus accommodation available for international students?
faq.a: Presidency University offers dedicated student housing on its Bengaluru campus. International students are encouraged to contact the Office of International Affairs for information on hostel availability, room types, and the booking process.

programs.name: B.Tech Computer Science & Technology (DevOps)

hero.description: Master the principles and tools that power modern software delivery. This four-year specialisation in DevOps engineering prepares you to design CI/CD pipelines, automate infrastructure, and manage cloud-native environments — skills that are critical to the way global technology organisations build and deploy software today.

overview: DevOps has become a foundational practice in how organisations build, test, deploy, and maintain software systems at scale. This specialisation integrates the principles of software development and IT operations, equipping graduates with the skills to create automated, reliable, and scalable delivery pipelines that accelerate the entire software development lifecycle

The curriculum covers CI/CD pipeline design, containerisation with Docker and Kubernetes, infrastructure automation using Terraform and Ansible, cloud computing on AWS and other platforms, monitoring and observability tools, and version control systems. Students develop proficiency in software development and project management methodologies, learning to streamline workflows and optimise collaboration between development and operations teams.

faq.q: How does DevOps differ from traditional software engineering?
faq.a: DevOps integrates software development with IT operations to accelerate delivery, improve reliability, and enable continuous improvement. While traditional software engineering focuses on coding and design, DevOps encompasses the entire pipeline from development through deployment and monitoring.

faq.q: What tools will I learn in this programme?
faq.a: : The curriculum covers Jenkins, Docker, Kubernetes, Ansible, Terraform, AWS, Git/GitHub, monitoring tools (Prometheus, Grafana), and CI/CD platforms — the same tools used by leading technology organisations globally.

faq.q: What are the eligibility requirements for international applicants?
faq.a: Applicants must have completed 10+2 or equivalent with Physics and Mathematics as compulsory subjects, along with Chemistry or Computer Science or related subjects, with at least 45% marks combined.

faq.q: : What is the demand for DevOps professionals?
faq.a: DevOps is one of the fastest-growing engineering disciplines globally. As organisations accelerate cloud migration and software delivery, demand for skilled DevOps engineers continues to outpace supply across all major technology markets.

faq.q: Can I pursue further studies after this programme?
faq.a: Yes. Graduates are well-positioned for M.Tech or MS programmes in cloud computing, software engineering, or distributed systems at universities worldwide. The programme also provides a foundation for industry certifications that enhance career progression.

faq.q: Is on-campus accommodation available for international students?
faq.a: Presidency University offers dedicated student housing on its Bengaluru campus. International students are encouraged to contact the Office of International Affairs for information on hostel availability, room types, and the booking process.

programs.name: B.Tech Computer Science & Engineering (AI & ML)

hero.description: Step into the field that is reshaping every industry on theplanet. This four-year specialisation combines mathematics, programming, statistics, and software design to develop your ability to build intelligent systems that learn, reason, and adapt preparing you for high-impact careers in AI research, data science, and automation

overview: Artificial Intelligence is a branch of computer science focused on enabling machines to perform tasks that typically require human intelligence including rational thinking, perception, and decision-making. Machine Learning extends this by enabling systems to teach themselves from data, improving their performance without explicit programming. This programme fuses these disciplines into a comprehensive engineering education.

The curriculum combines core domains of mathematics, computer programming, machine learning algorithms, statistical modelling, and software design to develop the capacity to replicate and extend human cognitive processes omputationally. Students work with industry-standard platforms including TensorFlow, PyTorch, and cloud-based AI services, building competencies in deep learning, neural networks, NLP, computer vision, and reinforcement learning. Graduates are recruited by multinational IT companies, large Indian technology organisations, AI-focused product firms, and research institutions.

programs.name: B.Tech Computer Science & Engineering (Blockchain)

hero.description: Enter the world of decentralised technology that is transforming finance, supply chains, governance, and digital identity. This four-year specialisation combines computer science fundamentals with deep expertise in cryptography, distributed ledger technology, and smart contract development — preparing you for careers at the intersection of security, innovation, and digital transformation.

overview: Blockchain technology is a decentralised, distributed ledger system that securely records the provenance and transfer of digital assets. It has moved well beyond cryptocurrency to become a foundational technology across financial services, supply chain management, healthcare records, government services, and digital identity verification.

This specialisation combines core domains of mathematics,computer programming, software design, and information security to prepare graduates for roles as blockchain developers and solution architects. The curriculum covers smart contracts, decentralised applications (DApps), cryptographic protocols, consensus mechanisms, peer-to-peer networking, and leading blockchain platforms including Ethereum, Hyperledger, and Corda. Graduates are positioned for careers in Web3, FinTech, cyber security, and enterprise blockchain solutions with multinational IT companies and emerging technology firms.

faq.q: What makes blockchain a promising career field?
faq.a: Blockchain technology is being adopted across finance, supply chain, healthcare, government, and digital identity sectors worldwide. Demand for blockchain developers and architects continues to grow as organisations implement decentralised solutions for security, transparency, and efficiency.

faq.q: How does this specialisation differ from B.Tech CSE (Cyber Security)?
faq.a: While Cyber Security focuses on protecting systems and data from threats, Blockchain specialises in building decentralised, distributed systems using cryptographic protocols. Both share elements of cryptography and security, but Blockchain is oriented towards building new systems rather than defending existing ones

faq.q: What are the eligibility requirements for international applicants?

programs.name: B.Tech Computer Science & Engineering (Cyber Security)

hero.description: Defend the digital frontier. As cyber threats grow in scale and sophistication, organisations worldwide require skilled professionals who can protect systems, networks, and data. This four-year specialisation prepares you with the tools, techniques, and strategic thinking to safeguard critical digital infrastructure across industries.

overview: Cyber security is no longer optional — it is essential for every organisation, government, and institution operating in the digital age. This specialisation provides a comprehensive grounding in ethical hacking, digital forensics, penetration testing, cryptography, network security, and threat intelligence, equipping graduates with the skills to identify vulnerabilities, defend against attacks, and secure critical systems.

The curriculum integrates core computer science with specialised security modules, providing hands-on experience with industry-standard tools including Kali Linux, Metasploit, Wireshark, and SIEM solutions. Students also develop proficiency in Python, C/C++, and SQL as applied to security contexts. With cybersecurity professionals in high demand across government agencies, financial institutions, technology companies, and defence organisations worldwide, this programme offers strong career prospects and global mobility.

programs.name: B.Tech Computer Science & Engineering (Data Science)

hero.description: Turn raw data into strategic advantage. This four-year specialisation equips you to extract knowledge, enable discovery, and drive decisions from complex datasets — combining statistics, computer science, and domain expertise to prepare you for one of the most sought-after roles in the global economy.

overview: Data science is an interdisciplinary field focused on extracting knowledge and enabling discovery from complex data. It represents a fusion of principles from statistics and computer science, applied in domain-specific contexts across healthcare, finance, retail, manufacturing, and beyond. This specialisation combines mathematics, computer programming, and software design to equip graduates with the skills to manage and interpret digital data at scale.

The curriculum covers the full data pipeline — from collection and preprocessing to analysis, modelling, and visualisation — using industry-standard tools including Python, R, TensorFlow, Hadoop, Apache Spark, and advanced analytics frameworks. Students build competencies in machine learning, AI, deep learning, cloud computing, feature engineering, and data visualisation that align directly with what global employers demand.

faq.q: How does Data Science differ from Big Data?
faq.a: Data Science focuses on extracting insights and building predictive models from data, while Big Data emphasises the infrastructure and tools needed to process and manage extremely large datasets. Both specialisations share common tools but differ in emphasis.

programs.name: B.Tech Computer Science & Engineering (Internet of Things)

hero.description: Connect the physical and digital worlds. This four-year specialisation prepares you to design, build, and manage the interconnected systems that power smart cities, intelligent manufacturing, healthcare monitoring, and connected devices  developing skills at the intersection of hardware, software, and data.

overview: The Internet of Things represents one of the most transformative technology trends shaping industries today. From smart homes and wearable health monitors to industrial automation and intelligent transportation systems, IoT connects physical devices to digital networks, enabling data-driven decision-making at unprecedented scale.

This specialisation combines core computer science with deep expertise in embedded systems, sensor networks, wireless communications, cloud platforms, edge computing, and data analytics. Students design and build IoT solutions using industry-standard platforms and tools, gaining hands-on experience with hardware-software integration, real-time data processing, and system security. The programme prepares graduates for careers in smart automation, connected devices, industrial IoT, and emerging technology sectors.

programs.name: B.Tech Computer Engineering

hero.description: Master the full spectrum of computing from hardware architecture to intelligent software. Combine embedded systems, chip-level design, AI, cybersecurity, and IoT engineering into integrated solutions that power modern technology infrastructure

overview: : The B.Tech in Computer Engineering sits at the intersection of computer science and electrical engineering, training students to work fluently across both hardware infrastructure and software intelligence. The programme covers embedded systems, processor architecture, software development, artificial intelligence, cybersecurity, and the Internet of Things — producing engineers who understand computing from silicon to cloud.

With the worldwide demand for professionals who can bridge hardware and software growing rapidly, this programme positions graduates for roles in semiconductor design, systems engineering, embedded development, and full-stack product creation. Hands-on laboratory work with microprocessors, FPGAs, and development boards ensures students graduate with practical, industry-ready competencies.

faq.q: How does Information Science & Engineering differ from Computer Science & Engineering?
faq.a: Computer Engineering has a stronger emphasis on hardware architecture, embedded systems, and the integration of hardware with software. CSE focuses more broadly on computing theory, algorithms, and software development. CE is ideal for students interested in building the physical systems that power computing.

faq.q: What are the eligibility requirements for international applicants?
faq.a: AApplicants must have completed 10+2 or equivalent with Physics and Mathematics as compulsory subjects, achieving at least 45 percent marks combined. International qualifications are evaluated on a case-by-case basis by the admissions team

faq.q: What types of organisations recruit Computer Engineering graduates?
faq.a: Semiconductor companies, embedded systems firms, product development organisations, IT services companies, systems integrators, and technology startups all actively recruit CE graduates.

faq.q: Does the programme include practical hardware experience?
faq.a: Yes. Extensive laboratory work with microprocessors, FPGAs, development boards, and embedded platforms ensures students gain substantial hands-on hardware experience alongside software development skills.

faq.q: Can graduates pursue postgraduate studies internationally?
faq.a: Yes. The programme provides a strong foundation for MS or M.Tech programmes in computer engineering, embedded systems, VLSI design, or related fields at universities globally.

faq.q: Is on-campus accommodation available for international students?
faq.a: Presidency University provides dedicated student housing on its Bengaluru campus. International students should contact the Office of International Affairs for details.

programs.name: B.Tech Robotics & Artificial Intelligence

hero.description: Build the intelligent machines of tomorrow. Combine AI, machine learning, deep learning, autonomous navigation, computer vision, and IoT with hands-on robotics engineering to shape the future of automation across industries worldwide.

overview: AI-driven robotics is transforming healthcare, education, automotive manufacturing, agriculture, and industrial production at an unprecedented pace. The global demand for professionals who can design, programme, and deploy autonomous systems is accelerating across every sector. This programme positions students at the forefront of this transformation, combining core robotics engineering with cutting-edge artificial intelligence.

The curriculum covers AI, machine learning, deep learning, autonomous navigation and vehicle systems, computer vision, IoT integration, and industrial automation. Students work in state-of-the-art robotics laboratories with industry-standard platforms, building complete autonomous systems from concept through prototype to deployment. The programme develops both the theoretical foundations and practical engineering skills required for careers in robotics and intelligent systems globally.

faq.q: What makes Robotics & AI different from a standard AI programme?
faq.a: This programme integrates physical robotics engineering with AI software. You will work with actual robots, drones, and embedded systems alongside machine learning and computer vision, not just software-based AI development.

faq.q: What are the eligibility requirements for international applicants?

faq.q: Is this programme suitable for students interested in self-driving vehicles?

overview: The B.Tech in Artificial Intelligence & Data Science develops expertise across four interconnected domains: data science, machine learning, deep learning, and artificial intelligence. Building on a core computer science foundation, the programme equips students to extract actionable insights from complex datasets and build intelligent systems that drive data-informed decision-making across sectors from finance and healthcare to retail and manufacturing.

The curriculum covers AI algorithms, machine learning techniques, deep learning architectures, big data processing, cloud computing, feature engineering, data preprocessing, data visualisation, and natural language processing. Students gain proficiency with Python, R, TensorFlow, Hadoop, and Apache Spark through hands-on projects addressing real-world problems.

faq.q: How do international students apply for this programme?
faq.a: : International applicants should visit the Presidency University International admissions portal or contact the Office of International Affairs directly. The enrolment process typically involves submitting academic transcripts, proof of English language proficiency, and any relevant entrance examination scores.

faq.q: Are entrance examinations required for admission?
faq.a: Applicants for M.Tech programmes generally need to present scores from recognised postgraduate engineering entrance assessments such as GATE. International applicants should check with the admissions team whether alternative qualifications or examinations are accepted.

faq.q: Can students without extensive programming experience succeed?
faq.a: Yes. While a foundational understanding of programming is helpful, the programme builds competencies progressively. Students committed to developing skills in Python, data handling, statistics, and algorithmic thinking will find the programme accessible.

faq.q: Which organisations recruit graduates from this programme?
faq.a: Graduates are sought by major IT companies, AI-focused product firms, data science consultancies, deep-tech startups, government research bodies, and organisations in the BFSI sector. Both multinational corporations and international firms recruit from Presidency placement drives.

faq.q: What is the typical starting salary for graduates?
faq.a: Starting compensation typically falls within INR 6 to 12 lakhs per annum for positions in India. International roles may offer different compensation structures.

faq.q: : Is on-campus accommodation available for international students?
faq.a: Presidency University offers dedicated student housing on its Bengaluru campus. International students are encouraged to contact the Office of International Affairs for information on hostel availability, room types, and the booking process.

programs.name: M.Tech Computer Science & Engineering (Data Science)

hero.description: Advance your expertise in big data analytics, machine learning, AI, and cloud computing. This research-intensive postgraduate programme prepares engineers for leadership roles in data-driven technology and decision systems.

overview: Designed for engineers seeking advanced expertise in data analytics, machine learning, and AI-driven decision systems, this M.Tech programme combines rigorous academic coursework with research-oriented learning. Students develop the theoretical depth and practical skills needed to lead data science initiatives in technology companies, research organisations, and consulting firms worldwide.

SThe curriculum encompasses big data analytics, machine learning, AI, deep learning, cloud computing, and statistical modelling. Students work with Python, R, TensorFlow, Hadoop, Apache Spark, and cloud-native data platforms. Research dissertations in Semesters III and IV address real-world data challenges, producing original contributions to the field.

programs.name: Ph.D in Computer Science Engineering

hero.description: Conduct original research at the frontiers of computing. This doctoral programme provides the academic framework, faculty mentorship, and research infrastructure to produce knowledge that advances the field and shapes the future of technology.

overview: The Ph.D. in Computer Science Engineering offers researchers the opportunity to conduct original, sustained investigation into critical areas of computing science and technology. Research domains span artificial intelligence, machine learning, data science, cyber security, blockchain, Internet of Things, cloud computing, software engineering, and computational theory. The programme is designed to produce scholars whose contributions advance both academic knowledge and technological practice.

Doctoral candidates work under the supervision of experienced faculty members with active research profiles and international publication records. The university provides access to state-of-the-art computing laboratories, high-performance infrastructure, and research networks connecting Presidency scholars with national and international academic communities. Candidates are supported through the dissertation process with structured milestones, review panels, and publication guidance

faq.q: What are the admission requirements for international doctoral candidates?
faq.a: Applicants typically need a qualifying postgraduate degree (M.Tech, ME, or equivalent) in computer science or a related discipline. Specific eligibility criteria, entrance requirements, and documentation should be confirmed with the Office of International Affairs.

faq.q: How long does the doctoral programme take to complete?
faq.a: The typical duration is 3 to 5 years depending on the research scope and progress. Full-time registration is standard, with structured milestones and review panels tracking progress throughout the programme.

faq.q: Are research fellowships available for international students?
faq.a: Fellowship availability and terms should be confirmed with the Research and Innovation Council. Some funded research positions may be available depending on the research domain and faculty requirements.

faq.q: Can I pursue part-time doctoral research?
faq.a: Part-time registration options may be available for working professionals. Candidates should inquire with the School regarding specific arrangements and eligibility for part-time enrolment.

faq.q: What publication support is provided?
faq.a: The programme provides guidance on targeting appropriate journals, preparing manuscripts, navigating the peer review process, and funding for conference attendance and publication fees.

faq.q: : Is on-campus accommodation available for international students?
faq.a: Presidency University offers student housing. Doctoral candidates should contact the Office of International Affairs for details on accommodation options and availability.

hero.description: : Accelerate your data career with focused, practical training. Master the theory and application of data analytics, processing, and visualisation using the tools and platforms that power data-driven organisations in finance, healthcare, retail, and technology.

overview: This diploma programme combines essential theory with intensive practical skills development in big data technologies. The curriculum covers machine learning, cloud computing, distributed processing with Hadoop and Spark, and industry-expert-designed modules that prepare graduates for immediate contribution to data-driven organisations worldwide.

Students work across the full data lifecycle from data ingestion and storage through processing, analysis, and visualisation. The programme delivers proficiency in Hadoop, Spark, Python, R, SQL, Power BI, Tableau, and cloud data platforms on AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud Platform, giving graduates a comprehensive and immediately deployable skill set.

faq.q: What is the difference between the PG Diploma in Big Data and the M.Tech in Data Science?
faq.a: The PG Diploma is a shorter, practice-focused programme emphasising hands-on big data tools and deployment skills. The M.Tech is a longer, research-intensive degree with deeper theoretical foundations and a dissertation component. The diploma is ideal for professionals seeking rapid skill development; the M.Tech suits those pursuing research or advanced academic credentials.

faq.q: What are the eligibility requirements?
faq.a: : Applicants typically need a recognised undergraduate degree in engineering, computer science, or a related discipline. Specific eligibility criteria for international applicants should be confirmed with the admissions team.

faq.q: AIs prior programming experience required?
faq.a: Familiarity with at least one programming language is recommended. The programme covers Python, R, and SQL from foundational levels, but prior exposure to programming concepts will help students progress more quickly.

faq.q: Which organisations hire PG Diploma graduates?
faq.a: IT services companies, data analytics firms, e-commerce organisations, financial institutions, healthcare providers, and technology startups actively seek professionals with big data skills.

faq.q: Can I pursue further studies after completing the diploma?
faq.a: Yes. The diploma can serve as a stepping stone to M.Tech or MS programmes in data science, big data, or related fields. Some graduates also pursue professional certifications to complement their diploma qualification.

faq.q: : Is on-campus accommodation available for international students?
faq.a: Presidency University provides student housing. Contact the Office of International Affairs for details..

programs.name: B.Tech Electronics & Communication Engineering

hero.description: Build a strong foundation in computing, AI, and modern software technologies.

overview: The B.Tech in Electronics & Communication Engineering explores the design, development, and deployment of electronic devices, circuits, and communication systems that underpin modern life. From mobile networks and satellite communications to embedded controllers and IoT devices, the programme builds expertise across analogue and digital electronics, signal processing, wireless systems, VLSI technology, and microcontroller-based design. Students gain fluency in both hardware and software aspects of electronic engineering through an integrated curriculum of theory, laboratory practice, and project work.

Bengaluru’s position as a centre for semiconductor manufacturing, telecom R&D, and electronics innovation gives students proximity to major employers and research organisations. The programme incorporates industry-oriented certifications, collaborative projects, and internship placements that connect classroom learning to professional practice. Graduates emerge prepared for roles in telecommunications, semiconductor design, consumer electronics, aerospace, defence, and automation industries worldwide.

faq.q: What is eligibility?

faq.q: How do international students apply for the B.Tech CSE programme?
faq.a: International applicants should visit the Presidency University International admissions portal or contact the Office of International Affairs directly. The process typically involves submitting academic transcripts, proof of English language proficiency, and relevant entrance examination scores. Admission considers JEE Main, KCET, COMEDK, or the university’s own evaluation test.

faq.q: What entrance examinations are accepted?
faq.a: The university accepts JEE Main, JEE Advanced, Karnataka CET (KCET), COMEDK UGET, and the Presidency University Entrance Examination Test (PUEET). International applicants should check with the admissions team whether alternative qualifications are accepted.

faq.q: What career prospects are available after graduation?
faq.a: Graduates find roles as software developers, data scientists, AI engineers, cybersecurity specialists, cloud architects, and more across multinational IT companies, product firms, start-ups, government research bodies, and consulting organisations. The university’s placement cell maintains strong industry connections with regular on-campus recruitment drives.

faq.q: Is the B.Tech CSE programme in demand globally?
faq.a: Yes, B.Tech in Computer Science & Engineering remains one of the most in-demand degrees worldwide due to the growing need for tech professionals across every industry. Graduates are hired for roles spanning software development, data science, AI, and cybersecurity, with strong salary prospects and global career mobility.

faq.q: Is on-campus accommodation available for international students?
faq.a: Presidency University offers dedicated student housing on its Bengaluru campus. International students are encouraged to contact the Office of International Affairs for information on hostel availability, room types, and the booking process.

programs.name: B.Tech Electrical & Electronics Engineering

hero.description: Power the world’s critical infrastructure. This four-year programme covers electrical machines, power systems, control engineering, and electronics — preparing you for careers in energy generation, distribution, automation, and the growing electric vehicle sector.

overview: The B.Tech in Electrical & Electronics Engineering offers a distinctive blend of electrical, electronics, and computer-related coursework that prepares graduates for careers across power systems, automation, renewable energy, and electronics. The curriculum covers foundation courses in electrical machines, control systems, power electronics, microprocessors, programming, and energy systems — equipping students with the versatility to work across both traditional electrical engineering and emerging technology domains.

India’s rapidly expanding power sector, its ambitious renewable energy targets, and the accelerating adoption of electric vehicles make this a programme with strong long-term career prospects. Students benefit from laboratory facilities that simulate real-world electrical systems, alongside internship opportunities with power utilities, automation firms, and electronics manufacturers. The programme builds graduates who can design, implement, and manage the electrical and electronic systems that underpin modern infrastructure, industry, and transportation.

faq.q: What is the difference between EEE and ECE at Presidency?
faq.a: EEE focuses on electrical power generation, distribution, control systems, and energy management. ECE concentrates on electronic devices, communication systems, signal processing, and VLSI design. EEE graduates typically enter power, energy, and automation sectors, while ECE graduates tend toward telecom, semiconductor, and electronics industries.

faq.q: Is EEE relevant for the electric vehicle industry?
faq.a: Highly relevant. EEE graduates bring expertise in power electronics, motor control, battery management systems, and electrical drivetrain design — all critical competencies for the rapidly growing electric vehicle sector in India and globally.

faq.q: Can EEE graduates work in the renewable energy sector?
faq.a: Yes. The programme covers power systems, energy conversion, and grid management — all foundational to careers in solar energy, wind power, energy storage, and sustainable infrastructure development.

faq.q: Are government sector jobs accessible to EEE graduates?
faq.a: Yes. EEE graduates are eligible for Indian Engineering Services (IES) examinations and recruitment by public sector undertakings including BHEL, NTPC, ONGC, Power Grid Corporation, and ISRO. International students should check specific eligibility requirements for government

faq.q: What programming languages are covered in the EEE curriculum?
faq.a: The programme includes C, C++, Python, and MATLAB for tasks including automation, system modelling, simulation, data analysis, and embedded systems programming.

faq.q: Is on-campus accommodation available for international students?
faq.a: Presidency University provides student housing on its Bengaluru campus. Contact the Office of International Affairs for information on availability and booking.

programs.name: B.Tech VLSI

hero.description: Design the chips powering tomorrow’s technology. This specialised four-year programme focuses on integrated circuit design, semiconductor physics, FPGA prototyping, and electronic system verification — preparing you for careers at the heart of the global semiconductor industry.

overview: The B.Tech in VLSI (Very Large Scale Integration) provides focused training in the design, verification, and fabrication of integrated circuits and electronic systems. The curriculum covers semiconductor device physics, microelectronics, IC fabrication technology, CMOS circuit design, ASIC design, design verification, and FPGA-based embedded systems — all taught using industry-leading EDA (Electronic Design Automation) tools. This programme directly addresses the growing global demand for chip designers and verification engineers as the semiconductor industry expands across consumer electronics, automotive, healthcare, telecommunications, and computing.

Students progress from foundational electronics and programming in early semesters to advanced topics including digital and analogue VLSI design, HDL-based system design, microcontroller architectures, and signal processing. Laboratory work with tools from Cadence, Synopsys, Xilinx, and Mentor Graphics provides hands-on experience with the same platforms used by professional chip design teams. Bengaluru’s established semiconductor ecosystem — home to design centres for major global chipmakers — gives students proximity to internship and employment opportunities in the industry.

faq.q: What is the difference between B.Tech VLSI and B.Tech ECE at Presidency?
faq.a: B.Tech VLSI is a specialised programme focusing specifically on integrated circuit design, semiconductor technology, and chip verification. ECE provides broader coverage of electronics and communication systems including VLSI as one component alongside wireless communication, signal processing, and IoT. VLSI graduates typically enter semiconductor-focused roles.

faq.q: Which EDA tools are used in the VLSI programme?
faq.a: Students work with industry-standard tools including Cadence Virtuoso, Synopsys Design Compiler, Xilinx Vivado, and Mentor Graphics platforms for circuit design, simulation, synthesis, and verification.

faq.q: Is the VLSI programme relevant given the growth of semiconductor manufacturing in India?
faq.a: Highly relevant. India’s semiconductor industry is expanding significantly with new fabrication and design facilities being established. The demand for trained VLSI designers and verification engineers is projected to grow substantially, making this one of the most career-strategic engineering specialisations available.

faq.q: What salary range can VLSI graduates expect?
faq.a: Starting compensation for VLSI roles in India typically ranges from INR 4–8 LPA, with experienced professionals earning significantly more. International positions in semiconductor firms may offer different compensation structures depending on role and location.

faq.q: Can VLSI graduates pursue higher studies?
faq.a: Yes. B.Tech VLSI graduates are well-positioned for M.Tech and MS programmes in VLSI design, embedded systems, semiconductor technology, and related fields at institutions in India and internationally. The programme also provides a foundation for doctoral research in semiconductor technology.

faq.q: Is on-campus accommodation available for international students?
faq.a: Presidency University offers dedicated student housing. International students should contact the Office of International Affairs for details on availability and booking.

programs.name: B.Tech Civil Engineering

hero.description: Shape the built environment. This four-year programme covers structural design, geotechnical engineering, transportation systems, and sustainable construction — preparing you to plan and build the infrastructure that connects communities and drives economic development.

overview: The B.Tech in Civil Engineering provides comprehensive training in the planning, design, construction, and maintenance of physical infrastructure. The programme covers structural engineering, geotechnical engineering, transportation engineering, water resources, environmental engineering, and construction management. Students develop proficiency in mathematics, applied sciences, and engineering technology to analyse and design structures, systems, and processes that serve society. The curriculum combines classroom instruction with laboratory practice, site visits, and design projects that simulate professional civil engineering challenges.

India’s massive infrastructure investment programme — spanning highways, railways, smart cities, metro systems, airports, and urban development — creates sustained demand for qualified civil engineers. International students gain exposure to large-scale construction practices, emerging sustainable building technologies, and project management methodologies. The programme prepares graduates for careers with construction firms, consulting practices, government agencies, infrastructure developers, and international engineering organisations.

faq.q: What software tools are taught in the civil engineering programme?
faq.a: Students develop proficiency in AutoCAD, STAAD.Pro, ETABS, Revit BIM, and GIS mapping software — all industry-standard tools used by professional civil engineering firms.

faq.q: Are field visits and practical training included?
faq.a: Yes. The programme incorporates regular site visits, industrial training placements, and field projects that expose students to real-world construction processes, project management, and site engineering practice.

faq.q: Is civil engineering relevant in the age of AI and automation?
faq.a: Strongly relevant. Civil engineering is incorporating AI for structural health monitoring, BIM for digital construction, drones for surveying, and computational modelling for design optimisation. The profession is evolving, and the foundational skills remain essential.

faq.q: What is the placement scenario for civil engineering graduates?
faq.a: Graduates are recruited by construction companies, consulting firms, infrastructure developers, government agencies, and real estate organisations. Companies such as L&T, Tata Projects, and other national firms participate in campus recruitment drives.

faq.q: Can international students access study-abroad programmes?
faq.a: Yes. Civil engineering students have participated in exchange programmes at institutions including the University of Manchester and other partner universities. The Office of International Affairs facilitates these opportunities.

programs.name: B.Tech Mechanical Engineering

hero.description: Engineer the machines and systems that drive industry. This four-year programme covers thermodynamics, fluid mechanics, machine design, manufacturing, and automation — preparing you for careers across automotive, aerospace, energy, robotics, and advanced manufacturing sectors.

overview: The B.Tech in Mechanical Engineering delivers a comprehensive foundation across the core domains of mechanical science — thermodynamics, fluid mechanics, machine design, manufacturing processes, materials science, and mechatronics. The curriculum blends fundamental theory with hands-on experience in product design, prototyping, computer-aided engineering, and industrial automation. Students develop the analytical, computational, and design skills needed to conceive, develop, and manufacture mechanical systems ranging from precision components to large-scale industrial machinery.

Bengaluru’s diverse industrial landscape — spanning automotive manufacturing, aerospace, heavy engineering, robotics, and clean energy — provides students with direct access to internship and employment opportunities across multiple sectors. The programme includes laboratory work with CNC machines, 3D printers, CAD/CAM software, and simulation tools, along with industrial visits that connect classroom concepts to professional engineering practice. Graduates emerge as versatile engineers capable of contributing to manufacturing, product development, energy systems, and emerging technology domains worldwide.

Design solutions for complex problems including mechanical components and processes that meet specified requirements while considering health, safety, and environmental factors.
Conduct investigations using research-based methods including experimental design, data analysis, and information synthesis.
Select and apply appropriate techniques, resources, and modern tools to complex mechanical engineering activities with understanding of limitations.

PSOs.title: Design & Manufacturing Competence
PSOs.text: Ability to design, analyse, and manufacture mechanical systems and components using modern CAD/CAM tools and manufacturing processes.

PSOs.title: Thermal & Fluid Systems Analysis
PSOs.text: Ability to analyse and optimise thermal systems, fluid mechanics applications, and energy conversion processes for industrial applications.

PSOs.title: Automation & Mechatronics Integration
PSOs.text: Ability to integrate mechanical systems with electronic controls, automation technologies, and robotics for advanced engineering applications.

faq.q: Is mechanical engineering still relevant with the rise of software and AI?
faq.a: Highly relevant. Mechanical engineering is evolving through integration with AI, robotics, and automation. Modern mechanical engineers work with 3D printing, computational design, autonomous systems, and smart manufacturing — making it one of the most adaptable and enduring engineering disciplines.

faq.q: What is the mechatronics specialisation?
faq.a: The mechatronics specialisation combines mechanical engineering with electronics, control systems, and computer science. It prepares graduates for roles in robotics, automation, smart manufacturing, and Industry 4.0 applications.

faq.q: Which companies recruit mechanical engineering graduates from Presidency?
faq.a: Recruiters include automotive manufacturers such as TVS Motors, engineering conglomerates such as L&T, aerospace companies, energy firms, and manufacturing organisations. Both national and international employers participate in placement drives.

faq.q: What laboratory facilities are available?
faq.a: The department maintains laboratories for manufacturing processes, fluid mechanics, thermodynamics, materials testing, CNC machining, 3D printing, and CAD/CAM — all equipped with industry-standard machinery and software.

faq.q: Can mechanical engineering graduates pursue higher studies abroad?
faq.a: Yes. The B.Tech degree is well-recognised internationally. Graduates pursue M.S. and M.Tech programmes at institutions worldwide. The Office of International Affairs supports students exploring international academic pathways.

faq.q: Is on-campus accommodation available for international students?
faq.a: Presidency University provides student housing. Contact the Office of International Affairs for details.

programs.name: B.Tech Petroleum Engineering

hero.description: Engineer the machines and systems that drive industry. This four-year programme covers thermodynamics, fluid mechanics, machine design, manufacturing, and automation — preparing you for careers across automotive, aerospace, energy, robotics, and advanced manufacturing sectors.

overview: The B.Tech in Petroleum Engineering is a specialised multidisciplinary programme encompassing both upstream and downstream sectors of the oil and gas industry. The curriculum covers petroleum exploration, drilling engineering, reservoir management, production operations, digital oil fields, pipeline systems, refining technology, and health and safety — providing comprehensive training across the full petroleum value chain. Students develop expertise in subsurface evaluation, well system design, drilling operations, fluid handling, and resource valuation using geoscientific and engineering methods.

Presidency University is one of very few institutions in Bengaluru offering a dedicated petroleum engineering programme, giving students access to a distinctive academic experience alongside the city’s technology and industrial ecosystem. The department maintains specialised laboratory facilities including drilling simulation equipment, fluid mechanics testing apparatus, and reservoir modelling tools. Industry connections with major oil and gas companies including Shell and Schlumberger facilitate internship placements and graduate recruitment. The programme also incorporates project economics and resource valuation, equipping graduates with the technical and commercial skills demanded by the global energy sector.

faq.q: Is petroleum engineering a viable career choice given the energy transition?
faq.a: Yes. While the energy landscape is evolving, oil and gas remain significant global energy sources for the foreseeable future. Additionally, petroleum engineering skills in subsurface analysis, fluid dynamics, and project management are transferable to geothermal energy, carbon capture, and other sustainable energy technologies.

faq.q: Which companies recruit petroleum engineering graduates from Presidency?
faq.a: Major recruiters include Shell, Schlumberger, ONGC, IOC (Indian Oil Corporation), and Essar. Both multinational energy companies and national organisations participate in campus placement drives.

faq.q: Why is Presidency’s petroleum engineering programme distinctive?
faq.a: Presidency University is one of the very few institutions in Bengaluru offering a dedicated petroleum engineering programme. The department has established specialised labs with equipment not commonly found at other institutions in the region.

faq.q: What are the typical starting salaries for petroleum engineering graduates?
faq.a: Starting compensation typically ranges from INR 4–6 LPA for positions within India, with international roles and positions with major oil companies offering higher compensation depending on location and role.

faq.q: Is the programme GATE-aligned?
faq.a: Yes. The curriculum content is aligned with the GATE Petroleum Engineering syllabus, providing a strong foundation for students who wish to pursue postgraduate studies at IITs and other premier institutions through the GATE examination route.

faq.q: Is on-campus accommodation available for international students?
faq.a: Presidency University provides student housing. Contact the Office of International Affairs for details on availability and booking.

programs.name: M.Tech Embedded System & VLSI

hero.description: Advance your expertise in semiconductor design and embedded computing. This two-year postgraduate programme provides specialised training in VLSI design methodology, FPGA development, SoC architecture, and embedded systems — preparing you for senior roles in the global semiconductor industry.

overview: This two-year postgraduate programme focuses on the advances and applications of VLSI design methodology from a systems perspective. The curriculum covers EDA tool development, library design, analogue and mixed-signal design, semiconductor chip design, IC design and verification, FPGA development, and System-onChip (SoC) architecture. Students learn to analyse, design, and implement integrated circuits using industry-standard tools, developing the practical skills that make them immediately productive in professional chip design environments. The programme also enables students to formulate research problems and develop solutions in VLSI design and embedded systems.

The programme integrates core VLSI design principles with emerging trends in IoT, AI, and power-efficient electronics to meet current industry demands. Students access advanced laboratories equipped with tools for VLSI design, FPGA prototyping, circuit simulation, and embedded system testing. Faculty members bring deep experience in semiconductor design and embedded systems, providing both academic rigour and practical industry insight. Bengaluru’s concentration of semiconductor design centres — for companies ranging from Intel and Qualcomm to Indian design houses — creates a rich environment for internship placements and post-graduation employment.

faq.q: What are the eligibility requirements for international applicants?
faq.a: Applicants typically need a bachelor’s degree in engineering or technology (or equivalent) with a minimum of 55% marks. International applicants should confirm specific requirements with the admissions team. GATE scores are commonly accepted but alternative qualifications may be considered.

faq.q: What EDA tools and platforms are used in the programme?
faq.a: Students work with industry-standard tools including Cadence, Synopsys, Xilinx, and simulation platforms for VLSI design, verification, FPGA prototyping, and embedded system development.

faq.q: Is there a dissertation or internship component?
faq.a: Yes. The programme includes a substantial dissertation or industry internship component across the later semesters, allowing students to conduct in-depth research or gain professional experience.

faq.q: What is the demand for VLSI professionals in India?
faq.a: India’s semiconductor industry is experiencing significant growth with new fabrication facilities and design centres being established. The demand for trained VLSI designers and verification engineers is projected to increase substantially over the coming decade.

faq.q: Can graduates pursue doctoral research?
faq.a: The M.Tech programme provides a strong foundation for Ph.D. research in VLSI design, embedded systems, semiconductor technology, and related fields at institutions in India and internationally.

programs.name: M.Tech Building & Construction Technology

hero.description: Advance your expertise in semiconductor design and embedded computing. This two-year postgraduate programme provides specialised training in VLSI design methodology, FPGA development, SoC architecture, and embedded systems — preparing you for senior roles in the global semiconductor industry.

overview: This two-year postgraduate programme provides advanced knowledge in building and construction technology, covering structural systems, construction materials, project management, sustainability, and digital construction methodologies. The curriculum bridges the gap between traditional civil engineering education and the evolving demands of the modern construction industry, where Building Information Modelling (BIM), green building standards, and advanced materials are transforming how infrastructure is designed, built, and maintained.

India’s construction sector is one of the largest globally, driven by massive government investment in smart cities, housing, transportation infrastructure, and industrial development. International students gain access to large-scale construction projects and emerging practices in sustainable building, alongside academic instruction grounded in both theory and professional application. The programme prepares graduates for senior roles in construction management, structural consulting, real estate development, and infrastructure project delivery.

PSOs.title: Construction Project Delivery
PSOs.text: Skill in managing complex construction projects from planning through execution, incorporating quality control, safety, and sustainability standards.

highlights.title: Advanced Construction Curriculum
highlights.description: Covers structural systems, construction materials, project management, BIM, and green building technologies. .

highlights.title: India’s Construction Sector Growth BIM & Digital Construction Training Green Building & Sustainability Focus
highlights.description: Direct access to one of the world’s largest and fastest-growing construction markets with massive infrastructure investment. Hands-on experience with Building Information Modelling, computational design, and digital project delivery tools. Coursework addressing sustainable construction practices, energy-efficient building design, and environmental certification standards.

highlights.title: Research-Oriented Programme Industry-Connected Faculty Smart City Development Exposure
highlights.description: Dissertation component enabling original research contributions to building and construction technology. Academics with experience in construction consulting, structural analysis, and project delivery. Proximity to India’s smart city initiatives and urban development projects providing real-world context for academic study.

highlights.title: International Qualification Recognition
highlights.description: M.Tech degree recognised for professional practice and further study globally.

faq.q: What are the eligibility requirements for international applicants?
faq.a: Applicants typically need a bachelor’s degree in civil engineering, architecture, or a related discipline with a minimum of 50% marks. International applicants should confirm specific requirements with the admissions team.

faq.q: Is BIM training included in the programme?
faq.a: Yes. The programme incorporates Building Information Modelling as part of its digital construction methodology coursework, providing hands-on experience with BIM tools and workflows.

faq.q: What is the scope for construction technology professionals in India?
faq.a: India’s construction sector is experiencing massive growth driven by smart city development, housing projects, transportation infrastructure, and industrial expansion. The demand for professionals with advanced construction technology expertise is strong and projected to increase.

faq.q: Is there a dissertation or internship component?
faq.a: YesThe programme includes a dissertation or industry internship component allowing students to conduct research or gain professional experience in construction technology.

faq.q: Can graduates pursue doctoral research?
faq.a: Yes. The M.Tech provides a foundation for Ph.D. research in structural engineering, construction technology, sustainable building, and related fields.

faq.q: Is on-campus accommodation available for international students?
faq.a: Presidency University provides student housing. Contact the Office of International Affairs for details.

programs.name: Post Graduate Diploma in Drone Technology

title.normal: Post Graduate Diploma in Drone Technology

hero.description: Launch your career in unmanned aerial systems. This one-year programme — developed in partnership with Garuda Aerospace — covers drone design, operation, AI-driven data analysis, and regulatory compliance, preparing you for one of the fastest-growing technology sectors in aviation, agriculture, infrastructure, and environmental monitoring.

overview: This innovative one-year postgraduate diploma opens doors to the rapidly growing unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) industry. Developed in collaboration with Garuda Aerospace — a leading Indian drone company — the programme provides industry-grade training covering drone system design, aerodynamics, flight control, sensor integration, aerial surveying, AI-driven data processing, and regulatory compliance. Students train at a dedicated Centre of Excellence in Drone Technologies established through the university’s partnership with Garuda Aerospace, gaining access to real-world R&D opportunities and advanced prototyping facilities.

The programme addresses a growing skills gap as drone technology transforms industries including agriculture, logistics, infrastructure inspection, environmental monitoring, defence, and entertainment. Students progress from fundamental principles of UAV design through to advanced applications including photogrammetry, GIS-based analysis, 3D modelling, and autonomous flight systems. The curriculum incorporates hands-on field training in aerial surveying and asset inspection, alongside theoretical coursework in drone regulations, safety protocols, and commercial deployment strategies. Whether you are a professional looking to transition into UAV technology or a graduate seeking a career in emerging aerospace applications, this diploma provides focused, industry-ready skills.

eligibility.title: Eligibility Criteria
eligibility.description: Candidates must have completed 10+2 with Physics and Mathematics with a minimum of 45% marks.
eligibility.note: Eligibility may vary for international students.

PEOs.title: UAV Technical Expertise
PEOs.text: Prepare graduates with expertise in drone system design, operation, and maintenance across agriculture, infrastructure, and environmental applications.

PEOs.title: Technology Integration
PEOs.text: Develop graduates who integrate AI, machine learning, and GIS technologies to automate drone workflows and enhance decision-making.

PEOs.title: Responsible Practice
PEOs.text: Foster graduates who apply UAV solutions responsibly, adhering to global regulations, safety standards, and ethical practices.

PEOs.title: Entrepreneurship & Innovation
PEOs.text: Inspire graduates who lead or launch ventures in dronebased services, smart infrastructure, and urban air mobility.

POs.text: Design and assemble UAV systems incorporating airframe structures, propulsion, flight controllers, and sensor payloads.
Operate drones safely and effectively for aerial surveying, inspection, mapping, and data acquisition missions.
Apply AI and machine learning techniques to process, analyse, and interpret droneacquired data for actionable insights.
Navigate regulatory frameworks including DGCA regulations and international UAV compliance standards.
Recognise the need for continuous learning to stay current with rapidly evolving drone technologies and industry trends.

PSOs.title: UAV Building & Programming
PSOs.text: Ability to build and programme UAVs using microcontrollers, sensors, and wireless systems for advanced missions.

PSOs.title: Geospatial Applications
PSOs.text: Ability to apply photogrammetry, GIS, and remote sensing for real-world applications including urban planning and disaster response.

PSOs.title: AI-Powered Data Workflows
PSOs.text: Competence in implementing AI/ML for drone data workflows including 3D modelling, image classification, and analytics.

highlights.title: Garuda Aerospace Partnership
highlights.description: Centre of Excellence established through strategic MoU provides access to expert mentorship, real-world R&D, and industry-grade drone training.

highlights.title: Industry-Integrated Curriculum 3D Printing & Prototyping Lab
highlights.description: Courses co-developed with leading UAV companies ensuring relevance to market demands and immediate job readiness. Hands-on prototyping of custom drones and components fostering creativity, innovation, and technical expertise.

highlights.title: AI & Data Analytics Training Field Training Component
highlights.description: Modules covering AI-driven image processing, 3D modelling, and geospatial analytics for drone-acquired data. Practical aerial survey and asset inspection training using professional-grade drone equipment in real-world environments.

highlights.title: Regulatory & Safety Compliance Rapidly Growing Industry
highlights.description: Comprehensive coverage of DGCA regulations, international UAV standards, and flight safety protocols. India’s drone market is projected to grow substantially, driven by applications in agriculture, logistics, defence, and infrastructure.

highlights.title: Entrepreneurship Pathway
highlights.description: Skills and knowledge to launch ventures in drone delivery, environmental inspection, smart infrastructure, and aerial services.

faq.q: What is the eligibility for the PG Diploma in Drone Technology?
faq.a: Candidates need a recognised undergraduate degree with a minimum of 50% marks (45% for SC/ST candidates from Karnataka). The programme accepts graduates from engineering, science, and related disciplines.

faq.q: Is prior drone flying experience required?
faq.a: No. The programme covers drone operation from foundational principles through to advanced flight training. Prior experience is beneficial but not required for admission.

faq.q: What is the partnership with Garuda Aerospace?
faq.a: Presidency University has established a Centre of Excellence in Drone Technologies through a strategic MoU with Garuda Aerospace, one of India’s leading drone companies. This provides students with access to industry-grade training, mentorship, real-world projects, and advanced drone systems.

faq.q: Does the programme cover drone regulations?
faq.a: Yes. The curriculum includes comprehensive coverage of DGCA (Directorate General of Civil Aviation) regulations, international UAV compliance standards, safety protocols, and commercial drone licensing requirements.

faq.q: What sectors hire drone technology graduates?
faq.a: Graduates are employed in agriculture (crop monitoring, precision spraying), infrastructure (inspection, surveying), defence and security (surveillance), logistics (delivery operations), environmental monitoring, real estate, media production, and disaster management.

faq.q: Is on-campus accommodation available for international students?
faq.a: Presidency University provides student housing. Contact the Office of International Affairs for details on availability and booking.

cta.title: Your Future Starts Here
cta.subtitle: Join Presidency University and build your career in technology.

hero.description: Engineer Solutions for Tomorrow. Our engineering programs develop skilled professionals capable of designing, building, and innovating solutions to complex technical and societal challenges.

about.title: About the School
content: The School of Engineering encompasses multiple disciplines including mechanical, civil, electrical, petroleum, and specialized engineering fields. We emphasize practical skills, hands-on laboratory experience, and collaboration with industry to prepare engineers for real-world challenges.

vision.title: Vision
vision.description: To cultivate world-class engineers who drive innovation, sustainability, and progress through advanced technical knowledge and ethical practice.

mission.title: Mission
mission.description: To deliver comprehensive engineering education combining theoretical rigor with practical application, foster research and innovation, and develop engineers committed to sustainable development.

programSections.title: Undergraduate Programmes
programSections.subtitle: Build strong foundations in your field of interest

programSections.title: Postgraduate Programmes
programSections.subtitle: Advance your expertise with specialized studies

programSections.title: Doctoral & Diploma Programmes
programSections.subtitle: Pursue research and specialized professional training

hero.description: Step into the digital future with application-focused computing programmes. From software development and data science to artificial intelligence and cybersecurity, our school prepares international students with the practical skills and industry-ready expertise that technology employers seek worldwide.

about.title: Where Information Becomes Insight
content: The Presidency School of Information Science offers application-centred computing education through three undergraduate programmes and a postgraduate programme, each designed to produce graduates with strong programming, analytical, and software development skills. The school focuses on fields that define the modern technology landscape — data science, artificial intelligence, machine learning, cybersecurity, cloud computing, and full-stack development. With a curriculum structured around the Choice Based Credit System (CBCS) and Outcome Based Education (OBE), students build both breadth and depth across information technology disciplines.

The school has established collaborations with IITs and prominent industry partners for laboratory training, summer programmes, and industry-based certification. A partnership with a Switzerland-based organisation through the Global AI Hub has provided AI and data science certification training to over 2,800 students. Faculty members have been trained at IIT Madras in XR/AR and VR technologies, and the school is developing a Centre of Excellence in Extended Reality (XR). Over 100 research scholars currently pursue doctoral programmes within the school, and a Digital Forensics initiative is being developed in collaboration with the Criminal Investigation Department of Karnataka State. The school’s BCA programme has been ranked 27th nationally among the Top 100 BCA Colleges and Universities.

features.title: Global AI Hub Certification Partnership
features.description: Strategic collaboration with a Switzerland-based organisation providing AI and data science certification training to students, with over 2,800 certifications delivered.

features.title: IIT Collaboration & XR Centre of Excellence
features.description: Faculty trained at IIT Madras in extended reality technologies, with an XR Centre of Excellence under development and ongoing partnerships with premier Indian institutions.

features.title: Industry-Ready Skills Programme
features.description: The Super-60 advanced training programme identifies fast learners and provides specialised skill development including CODEATHON events and hands-on modern software tool training.

vision.title: Vision
vision.description: To be a value-based, practice-driven school of information science, committed to developing globally competent professionals dedicated to applying modern information science for social benefit.

mission.title: Mission
points: Cultivate a practice-driven environment with an information-technology-based pedagogy integrating theory and practice
Attract and nurture world-class faculty to excel in teaching and research in the information science domain
Establish state-of-the-art facilities for effective teaching and learning experiences
Promote interdisciplinary studies to nurture talent for global impact
Instil entrepreneurial and leadership skills to address social, environmental, and community needs

programPreview.title: About the School of Law
programPreview.subtitle: Building Future Legal Professionals
content: The Presidency School of Law is approved by the Bar Council of India.
Offers integrated law programmes and global exposure.

programTable.description: Five-year full-time programme with focus on legal excellence, research, and practical exposure.

faq.q: What is the difference between B.A. LL.B. and B.B.A. LL.B.?

programs.name: Bachelor of Computer Applications (BCA)

hero.description: Launch your career in software and technology. This three-year programme builds strong foundations in programming, databases, networking, and software development — equipping you with the practical application skills sought by the IT and ITES industries across India and internationally.

overview: The Bachelor of Computer Applications programme is designed to meet the skill requirements of the rapidly growing IT and ITES industries. The curriculum covers computer algorithms and programming, database management systems, computer networks, internet technologies, cloud computing, and mobile application development. Students progress from foundational topics in C programming, applied mathematics, and web development in the first year through Java, database administration, operating systems, and software engineering in the second year, culminating in cloud computing, machine learning, Android development, and a capstone project in the final year.

The programme follows a practice-driven approach, with students introduced to business problem-solving through coding platforms such as CodeChef and HackerRank from the first year. Laboratory sessions, real-world projects, internships, and industry certification pathways ensure that graduates possess both theoretical knowledge and the applied skills that employers value. Bengaluru’s technology ecosystem provides direct access to internship and employment opportunities with software companies, IT services firms, start-ups, and digital agencies.

Conduct investigations of complex problems using research-based methods, data analysis, and synthesis to provide valid conclusions.

Assess societal, health, safety, legal, and cultural issues relevant to professional engineering practice.

Apply ethical principles and commit to professional ethics, responsibilities, and norms of engineering practice.
Function effectively as an individual and as a member or leader in diverse and multidisciplinary teams.
Communicate effectively with the engineering community and society at large on complex engineering activities.
Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of engineering and management principles to manage projects in multidisciplinary environments.
Recognise the need for and engage in independent and lifelong learning in the broadest context of technological change.

faq.q: What is the difference between BCA and B.Tech in Computer Science?
faq.a: BCA is a three-year programme focused on computer applications, software development, and IT skills. B.Tech CSE is a four-year engineering degree with deeper coverage of hardware, mathematics, and engineering fundamentals. BCA is ideal for students seeking a faster path into the IT industry; B.Tech provides broader engineering foundations for those interested in research or engineering roles.

faq.q: What are the eligibility requirements for international applicants?
faq.a: Candidates must have passed 10+2 or an equivalent examination from any recognised board with a minimum of 40% marks in aggregate. International applicants should confirm specific documentation requirements with the Office of International Affairs.

faq.q: Do I need prior programming experience?
faq.a: No. The programme builds programming skills from foundational levels. Students with a basic interest in computers, technology, and logical thinking will find the curriculum accessible and progressively challenging.

faq.q: What is the Super-60 training programme?
faq.a: Super-60 is an advanced employability programme that identifies high-performing students in the sixth semester and provides specialised training designed to prepare them for higher-level career opportunities and premium salary packages.

faq.q: What are the typical starting salaries for BCA graduates?
faq.a: Starting compensation for BCA graduates in India typically ranges from INR 3–5 LPA. Graduates with specialised skills in data science, AI, or full-stack development may command higher starting packages. International compensation varies by location and role.

faq.q: Is on-campus accommodation available for international students?
faq.a: Presidency University provides student housing. Contact the Office of International Affairs for details on availability and booking.

programs.name: BBA (Bachelor of Business Administration)

hero.description: Build the management foundations for a versatile career in business. This three-year programme covers finance, marketing, human resource management, operations, and entrepreneurship — developing the analytical, communication, and leadership skills that organisations seek in tomorrow’s business professionals.

overview: The Bachelor of Business Administration programme covers the core disciplines of modern business — HR management, organisational behaviour, business communication, finance management, marketing, business law, accounting, strategic planning, international business, retail management, supply chain management, and operations management. The curriculum is designed to develop well-rounded business professionals who can identify management challenges, think strategically, and apply practical solutions across functional areas. Students gain exposure to both established management frameworks and emerging business practices through a blend of classroom instruction, case studies, business simulations, and corporate projects.

The programme embeds industry engagement throughout, with corporate internships, live projects, industrial visits, and startup incubation support forming integral parts of the learning experience. Certification pathways in digital marketing, financial modelling, business analytics, and leadership development complement the academic curriculum. Bengaluru’s thriving corporate ecosystem — home to multinational headquarters, consulting firms, financial institutions, and a vibrant start-up scene — provides students with unparalleled proximity to professional opportunities and industry networks.
